pi_items.source: RFC 793 Section 3.2 defines exactly 11 TCP connection states: LISTEN, SYN-SENT, SYN-RECEIVED, ESTABLISHED, FIN-WAIT-1, FIN-WAIT-2, CLOSE-WAIT, CLOSING, LAST-ACK, TIME-WAIT, and CLOSED (https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc793)

RFC 793 Section 3.2 (https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc793) and setup_task.sh lines 27-51 which cite RFC 793 verbatim. The metadata uses underscores instead of hyphens, and abbreviates 'SYN-RECEIVED' to 'SYN_RCVD', which matches the initial diagram at setup_task.sh line 139 (value="SYN_RCVD") but differs from RFC 793 standard naming.

pi_items.verified_value: No direct transition exists from ESTABLISHED to CLOSED in RFC 793
pi_items.source: RFC 793 defines three closure paths: (1) ESTABLISHED→FIN-WAIT-1→FIN-WAIT-2→TIME-WAIT→CLOSED (active close), (2) ESTABLISHED→CLOSE-WAIT→LAST-ACK→CLOSED (passive close), (3) ESTABLISHED→FIN-WAIT-1→CLOSING→TIME-WAIT→CLOSED (simultaneous close). The direct link is forbidden. Also verified in setup_task.sh which marks this transition in red (strokeColor=#FF0000) as an error to be removed.

pi_items.metadata_value: 6 initial states in diagram (implied from task description mentioning 5 missing)
pi_items.verified_value: 6 states present in initial diagram: CLOSED, LISTEN, SYN_SENT, SYN_RCVD, ESTABLISHED, FIN_WAIT_1
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 127-149 define 6 state cells in the initial diagram XML

pi_items.metadata_value: ESTABLISHED -> CLOSED transition exists and must be removed
pi_items.verified_value: ESTABLISHED -> CLOSED transition is present in initial diagram at setup_task.sh lines 199-208 (id="edge_error" in red)
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh lines 199-208 define edge_error from state_estab to state_closed with red color

pi_items.verified_value: Data is synthetic - created by setup_task.sh as an incomplete diagram to be completed by the agent
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates all data files programmatically (lines 15-214), not from external sources

pi_items.verified_value: RFC excerpt matches RFC 793 Section 3.2 exactly, including all 11 state definitions and Figure 6 reference
pi_items.source: Manual comparison of setup_task.sh lines 27-51 against https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc793

privileged_info_summary: The task is a synthetic diagram completion task based on RFC 793 TCP state machine. RFC 793 defines exactly 11 TCP connection states. The task requires completing an initially incomplete diagram by adding 5 missing states (FIN_WAIT_2, CLOSING, TIME_WAIT, CLOSE_WAIT, LAST_ACK) and removing the erroneous ESTABLISHED→CLOSED transition, which is forbidden by RFC 793 (all closure paths must pass through intermediate states). File paths and the RFC reference text are verified against RFC 793 specifications. One discrepancy: RFC 793 officially designates the state as 'SYN-RECEIVED' while the task metadata and initial diagram use the abbreviation 'SYN_RCVD'.
